features.featureTitle: In FloatingPhotoFrame.tsx and BaseTemplate.tsx, remove the decorative filigree frame/overlay SVG element that renders on top of or in the middle of the bride and groom photos. Replace it with a premium CSS border/boundary styled directly on the photo container — use a multi-layered border approach (e.g., a thick solid or double gold/saffron border with a subtle inset shadow and an outer glow) that frames the photo from the outside without any element overlapping the photo image itself. The photo must be fully visible at all times. The border style should feel premium and on-theme (gold tones, rounded or slightly ornate corners using border-radius or a clip-path, consistent with the Hindu wedding aesthetic). The floating 3D bobbing animation on the photo container should be retained.

Add a WhatsApp sharing button to the invitation page (InvitePage.tsx or ShareSection.tsx). The button should be visible on the published invitation page at /invite/:id. When clicked, it opens a WhatsApp share link in a new tab using the format https://wa.me/?text=<encoded message> where the message includes the couple names, wedding date, and the full shareable invitation URL. Style the button to match the app's premium theme (gold/saffron accent, Cormorant Garamond or Cinzel typography). Place it near the existing share/QR section or as a floating button.
